people_highlights.post:
For years, we’ve been building our cyber defense program on the principles of democratized access, iterative deployment, and ecosystem resilience. 

As model capabilities advance, our approach is to scale cyber defense in lockstep: broadening access for legitimate defenders while continuing to strengthen safeguards.
people_highlights.why: This post emphasizes OpenAI's strategy to enhance cybersecurity defenses in tandem with AI advancements, crucial for maintaining safety in an increasingly AI-driven world.
people_highlights.name: OpenAI

people_highlights.post: Would you mind running /feedback and sharing the id here next time you see Opus do something weird? Would be very helpful for debugging
people_highlights.engagement: Likes=18, Reposts=2, Quotes=2, Replies=15, Bookmarks=5, Views=9116
people_highlights.why: This tweet highlights Anthropic's proactive approach to debugging and improving their AI model Opus based on user feedback.
people_highlights.name: Boris Cherny

people_highlights.post:
Research we co-authored on subliminal learning—how LLMs can pass on traits like preferences or misalignment through hidden signals in data—was published today in @Nature. 

Read the paper: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10319-8?error=cookies_not_supported&code=1d522790-89a0-4256-a598-456c7ef81bfb
people_highlights.why: This publication in Nature underscores critical advancements in AI safety research, revealing how large language models can subtly transmit unintended traits, which is vital for developing more reliable AI systems.
people_highlights.name: Anthropic

people_highlights.post:
Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS is our most controllable text-to-speech model yet.

With new Audio Tags, you can easily direct vocal style, delivery, and pace through text commands. 🧵
people_highlights.why: This announcement highlights significant improvements in AI text-to-speech technology, enhancing controllability for various applications.
people_highlights.name: Google DeepMind

people_highlights.post: Download or update the Claude desktop app to get started: https://claude.com/download Explore everything that's new: https://claude.com/product/claude-code
people_highlights.why: This post provides a call to action for users to experience the newly redesigned Claude Code desktop app, highlighting advancements in AI-assisted coding tools.
people_highlights.name: Claude

people_highlights.post: You did it! This AWS accomplishment reflects your passion and expertise. 🙌
people_highlights.why: This post celebrates user achievements in AWS certifications, highlighting the company's commitment to professional development in cloud technology.
people_highlights.name: AWS

people_highlights.post:
🚀 Today we're announcing Amazon Bio Discovery, a new AI-powered application designed to help scientists design and test novel drugs more quickly and confidently.

Scientists get access to AI models for drug discovery, and can converse naturally with an AI agent to select the right models for their research needs, optimize inputs, and evaluate drug candidates. They then send the top candidates to physical labs for testing, with results routing back for rapid iteration. Here's how we're making AI accessible to scientists in the lab ➡️
people_highlights.why: This announcement showcases AWS's innovation in applying AI to accelerate drug discovery, potentially revolutionizing scientific research and development.
people_highlights.name: Swami Sivasubramanian

people_highlights.why: This post shares access to Google DeepMind's new Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 model, enabling developers to explore advanced AI for enhancing robots' understanding and operation in the physical world.
people_highlights.name: Demis Hassabis

trending_posts.post:
Grok 4.20 Reasoning just took the #1 spot on the BridgeBench reasoning  benchmark. 🔥

Beating GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, Google Gemini and others.

Week after week, Grok keeps climbing across benchmarks. 🚀

trending_posts.author: @Google
trending_posts.name: Google
trending_posts.post:
Today, we’re introducing Skills in @GoogleChrome, a new way to build one-click workflows for your most frequently used AI prompts — like asking for ingredient substitutions to make a recipe vegan, generating side-by-side shopping comparisons across multiple tabs, or scanning long docs to get the info you need quickly.

When you write a prompt that you want to use again, you can save it as a Skill directly from your chat history. The next time you need it, select your saved Skill in Gemini in Chrome by typing forward slash ( / ) or clicking the plus sign ( + ) button, and your Skill will run on the page you’re viewing, along with any other tabs you select.

trending_posts.author: @GoogleResearch
trending_posts.name: Google Research
trending_posts.post: Meet Fabula: an interactive AI writing tool helping authors structure & refine stories. Co-designed with 42 expert writers, the demo showcases how convergent iteration supports creativity. Catch the demo at the Google booth at 10:30AM! #CHI2026

trending_posts.name: Roan
trending_posts.post:
This 2 hour Stanford lecture will teach you more about how LLMs like ChatGPT & Claude are built than most people working at top AI companies learn in their entire careers.

Bookmark this & give 2 hours today, no matter what. It'll be the most productive thing you do this week.

trending_posts.author: @AnthropicAI
trending_posts.name: Anthropic
trending_posts.post:
New Anthropic Fellows research: developing an Automated Alignment Researcher.

We ran an experiment to learn whether Claude Opus 4.6 could accelerate research on a key alignment problem: using a weak AI model to supervise the training of a stronger one.

https://www.anthropic.com/research/automated-alignment-researchers

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Meta AI Safety Report Sparks Discussion on Chemical and Biological Risks**: @summeryue0 shared Meta's Muse Spark Safety & Preparedness Report, highlighting pre-deployment assessments for risks like chemical/biological threats, cybersecurity, and loss of control. She noted, "Our assessment flagged potentially elevated chem/bio risk, so we implemented safeguards and validated mitigations before deployment - bringing residual risk to within acceptable levels."[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2044187757099233772) This has ignited debates on whether current AI safeguards are sufficient for advanced models.

• **Debate on AI Agents' Real Value and Future Potential**: @Chain_GPT announced an upcoming debate in their "AI After Dark" series, questioning "Why aren't AI agents delivering real value yet?" with guest @SecretNetwork. The post teases perspectives on what's working and not in AI agents, including how AIVM fits in, prompting discussions on the hype versus reality of AI agent capabilities.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2043640521420054907)

• **Lawsuit Over AI-Powered Surveillance Cameras in San Jose**: @NBCNews reported on a class action suit by three drivers against San Jose and its police department regarding Flock Safety's AI cameras that track vehicles. The controversy centers on privacy invasions, with the post stating, "a controversial surveillance tech company that uses AI and dedicated cameras to catalogue vehicles’ movements."[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2044491005785788517) This has fueled debates on AI ethics in public surveillance.

• **Call for Growth in AI Safety and Security Research**: @viemccoy responded to @ryan_kidd44's talk on expanding AI safety, emphasizing a "deficit of security and safety researchers" and urging, "The time to learn the field is now, there are tractable problems and we need your help."[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2044125035049476151) This highlights ongoing controversies about the shortage of experts in AI alignment and risk mitigation.

• **Open-Source AI Repo Emphasizes Safety and Interpretability**: @ihtesham2005 praised the "awesome-opensource-ai" repo for its depth, particularly the section on "AI Safety and Interpretability" with "alignment tooling, safety research repos, and interpretability frameworks." He noted, "Nobody else building these lists bothers," sparking discussions on the importance of open-source tools for addressing AI biases and ethical concerns.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2044008030275338341)
